summary: The 2026 Reedin Super Model refines the V3 platform with updated canopy shape and improved trailing edge tension for better stability and slightly more boost potential.
best_for: Performance freeriders wanting the latest Reedin freeride evolution

sources.summary: Early feedback suggests meaningful improvements in stability and slightly better boost over the V3, while maintaining the signature upwind performance.
Reviewer tests the updated Super Model and notes improved gust handling and better low-end compared to the V3.
Reedin continues to gain market share. Users see the Super Model as a strong alternative to the North Reach and Core Nexus.

structured_review.summary: The 2026 Reedin Super Model (marketed as 2025) is a thoroughly refined do-it-all freeride kite, built around a redesigned arc, pulley-less bridle, extra HF canopy panels, and battened trailing edge for a noticeably smoother, more direct feel. It is positioned as the kite for 95% of riders who want one quiver kite across disciplines. The main caveat is that the lineup now stops at 12m, with light-wind riders pushed to the separate Whisper Model.
pros: Pulley-less bridle delivers more direct, responsive bar feel
Extra HF panel on leading edge improves canopy twist and turning speed
Battened trailing edge produces exceptionally smooth, butter-like turns
Updated arc and repositioned bridles maintain full depower despite no pulley
Weight reduced across the kite without changing materials
Genuinely versatile — twin tip, foil, surf, and jumping all covered
cons: Lineup capped at 12m; light-wind riders must buy the separate Whisper Model
Pulley-less bridle is new and unproven long-term in the field
Performance riders wanting max speed and new-school response should look at the Hypermodel instead
Review is brand-hosted with Kevin Langeree — independent critical testing absent
structured_review.best_for: All-around freeriders who want one kite that handles twin tip, foil, casual jumping, and cruising across a wide wind range.
structured_review.not_for: Dedicated light-wind riders or aggressive new-school riders chasing maximum bar speed and performance headroom.
structured_review.rec_blurb: The smoothest all-rounder Reedin has built — one kite to rule almost every session.
